rationale: The cited paper appears only once, grouped with Kamakura and Wedel (1997, 2000), as an example of prior work that forms imputation groups via finite-mixture modeling. The citing article does not discuss the 2000 paper's specific method, assumptions, or findings separately, and it does not build its derivations or empirical procedure on that paper. Most detailed methodological contrast is made against other approaches (especially the 1997 model and hotdeck methods), so this citation functions as broad background positioning.
